## Conclusion
- The post itself clearly claims rising tanker-rerouting risk, but it does not externally confirm a formal closure or large-scale rerouting by itself.

## Confirmed
- The main post text argues that tanker rerouting risk is rising.
- The same-author thread explicitly says official confirmation of a closure has not yet been seen.
- The post includes screenshot-based image evidence that should stay separate from the original post wording.

## Unconfirmed
- The post does not externally confirm that rerouting is already happening at scale.
- The screenshots may point to supporting context, but they do not by themselves prove the underlying claims are current or representative.

## Risks
- Folding image summary into main post text would overstate what the author directly wrote.
- Treating the thread as external confirmation would blur social evidence with stronger-source verification.

## Next Step
- Bridge the key claim into a latest-event or news-index style verification pass and check whether stronger sources confirm actual rerouting or official shipping restrictions.
